summary: Split a large item assembly file into smaller item-collision-oriented assembly units. Most of the removed content from asm/melee/it/it_266F.s was reintroduced as new asm/melee/it/itcoll.s and asm/melee/it/it_2725.s files, then both new objects were added to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk immediately after it_266F.s.o. The PR had no body or review discussion, so the motivation is inferred from the title and diff: make the item collision region independently addressable for future decompilation while preserving link order.

key_files.role: source assembly file reduced by removing the itcoll/late item assembly ranges
key_files.evidence: 15949 deleted lines; after the split it retains earlier text through it_8026F9A0 and removes large text/data/bss/sdata chunks including itcoll.c strings.

key_files.role: new split-out item collision assembly unit
key_files.evidence: 3283 added lines; starts at it_8026F9AC and includes .text plus associated .data, .sdata, and .sdata2 symbols such as the asciz "itcoll.c".

key_files.role: new split-out later item assembly unit
key_files.evidence: 12685 added lines; starts at it_80272560 and includes .text plus associated .bss, .sdata, .sbss, and .sdata2 sections.
Added $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/it/itcoll.s.o and $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/it/it_2725.s.o immediately after $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/it/it_266F.s.o in TEXT_O_FILES.

what_changed: Removed a large contiguous assembly region from asm/melee/it/it_266F.s beginning after it_8026F9A0, including text functions starting at it_8026F9AC and later item functions through the high 0x8027CEFC range.

Created asm/melee/it/itcoll.s as a new assembly file with macros.inc, a .text section beginning at it_8026F9AC, and associated data sections containing item collision literals and tables, including "damage log over %d!!\n" and "itcoll.c".
Created asm/melee/it/it_2725.s as a second new assembly file with macros.inc, a .text section beginning at it_80272560, and associated non-text sections.
Updated obj_files.mk so the new split assembly objects are linked after it_266F.s.o and before later item objects.
smart_moves: Preserved apparent link order by inserting itcoll.s.o and it_2725.s.o directly after it_266F.s.o rather than elsewhere in TEXT_O_FILES.
Moved non-text sections along with the split text ranges, avoiding orphaned literals, bss, sdata, sbss, or sdata2 symbols in the old aggregate file.
Used a semantic file name, itcoll.s, for the region that evidence ties to itcoll.c via embedded string data, while leaving the less clearly named later range as address-style it_2725.s.

Kept the assembly functions globally labeled by address-style names such as it_8026F9AC and it_80272560, which makes the split mechanically safe even before full C decompilation or better symbol names are known.
decomp_lessons: When carving a large asm file into future-decompilation units, split at stable symbol boundaries and keep each new file self-contained with .include "macros.inc" and explicit section declarations.
For DOL matching, object-file order matters: new assembly objects should be added in the exact location corresponding to the original address order.
Do not move only .text; associated .data, .bss, .sdata, .sbss, and .sdata2 blocks must follow the source-file split so later C replacements can own the correct local constants and small-data symbols.
A file-local source string like "itcoll.c" is useful evidence for assigning an asm region to a semantic module name when symbol names are still mostly address-based.
A pure asm split can be a low-risk preparatory PR: it changes file boundaries and build objects without claiming to decompile or rename behavior.
naming_conventions: Semantic split file name used where evidence exists: asm/melee/it/itcoll.s corresponds to the embedded source string "itcoll.c".
Address-range style file name retained where semantics are weaker: asm/melee/it/it_2725.s begins at it_80272560.
Existing unknown item function names remain address-based, for example it_8026F9AC, it_8026FA2C, and it_80272560.
Project build objects follow $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/it/<file>.s.o entries in TEXT_O_FILES.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split assembly on global-symbol boundaries instead of editing instruction bodies.
Preserve original address order across object files: it_266F.s.o, then itcoll.s.o, then it_2725.s.o.
Carry over labels, branch targets, and address comments unchanged in the new files where visible in the diff.
Keep section alignment directives such as .balign with the moved data blocks.
Treat source-file assert strings and OSReport/assert literals as ownership clues for module partitioning.

review_feedback.note: No issue comments, review comments, or PR body were present in the slice, so there is no explicit rationale or requested change to extract.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile asm/melee/it/itcoll.s into C.
Search for subsequent renames of it_8026F9AC, it_8026FA2C, it_8026FC00, and it_80272560.
Inspect whether it_2725.s was later split again or assigned a semantic module name.
Compare link-order-sensitive asm split PRs that add new entries to TEXT_O_FILES near existing item objects.
